gloss.en: to leave someone or something for good

words.example: The baby had been abandoned by its mother.

gloss.en: left and no longer used or cared for

words.example: They found an abandoned house in the woods.

gloss.en: in a way that is not normal or usual

words.example: The weather was abnormally warm this winter.

gloss.en: relating to the original people of a land

words.example: We learned about aboriginal art and culture.

gloss.en: suddenly and in an unexpected way

words.example: The car stopped abruptly at the corner.

gloss.en: a person who is not present or away

words.example: The teacher made a list of the absentees.

gloss.en: total and complete

words.example: I've joined a class for absolute beginners.

gloss.en: to take in a liquid, gas, or substance

words.example: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air.

gloss.en: based on general ideas, not real things

words.example: It is hard to explain such abstract ideas.

gloss.en: connected with education and study

words.example: The school has high academic standards.

gloss.en: a way of pronouncing words from a place

words.example: She speaks English with a Scottish accent.

gloss.en: agreed or approved by most people

words.example: Children must learn acceptable behaviour.

gloss.en: how easy something is to reach or use

words.example: The website improved its accessibility for everyone.

gloss.en: by chance, in a way not planned

words.example: I accidentally hit him in the face.

gloss.en: to provide a place to stay or sit

words.example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

gloss.en: a place to live, work, or stay

words.example: We need to find cheap accommodation in the city.

gloss.en: to go somewhere with someone

words.example: His wife accompanied him on the trip.

gloss.en: to succeed in doing or finishing something

words.example: We accomplished the first part of the plan.

gloss.en: an arrangement to keep money at a bank

words.example: I don't have a bank account.

gloss.en: a person who keeps or checks accounts

words.example: We talked to the company's chief accountant.

gloss.en: the state of being exact or correct

words.example: They questioned the accuracy of the information.

gloss.en: correct and true in every detail

words.example: She gave an accurate description of the man.

gloss.en: in a correct and exact way

words.example: The article accurately reflects public opinion.

gloss.en: to say someone has done something wrong

words.example: They accuse him of stealing the money.

gloss.en: able to be done or reached

words.example: We set ourselves a realistic, achievable goal.

gloss.en: to accept that something is true

words.example: She refuses to acknowledge the need for reform.

gloss.en: to gain something by your own effort

words.example: She has acquired a good knowledge of English.

gloss.en: to make a device or process start working

words.example: The burglar alarm is activated by movement.

gloss.en: in an energetic or involved way

words.example: She is actively looking for a new job.

gloss.en: real or true; existing in fact

words.example: The actual cost was higher than we expected.

gloss.en: to change to suit a new situation

words.example: It's amazing how soon you adapt.

gloss.en: able to change to suit new conditions

words.example: A good leader must be adaptable to change.

gloss.en: a person who cannot stop a harmful habit

words.example: He became a drug addict at a young age.

gloss.en: unable to stop using or doing something

words.example: Many people are addicted to their phones.

gloss.en: being unable to stop a harmful habit

words.example: He sought help for his cocaine addiction.

gloss.en: more than the usual or first amount

words.example: The project will need additional funds.

gloss.en: as an extra thing; in addition

words.example: Additionally, the bus will run on Sundays.

gloss.en: details of where someone lives or works

words.example: What's your name and address?

gloss.en: enough or good enough for a purpose

words.example: They'll need an adequate supply of hot water.

gloss.en: in a way that is enough or good enough

words.example: Are you adequately insured?

gloss.en: to change something slightly to suit

words.example: Watch the bends and adjust your speed.

gloss.en: the work of running an organization

words.example: Administration costs are passed on to the customer.

gloss.en: deserving respect and praise
gloss.zh: 值得钦佩的；令人赞赏的

words.example: She showed admirable courage in the crisis.

gloss.en: in a way that shows respect or praise

words.example: He looked admiringly at her painting.

gloss.en: used to admit that something is true

words.example: Admittedly, the plan has some weak points.

gloss.en: to take another's child as your own

words.example: They decided to adopt a child.

gloss.en: taken into a family as a legal child

words.example: She told me she was an adopted child.

gloss.en: to love someone or something very much

words.example: The children adore their grandmother.

gloss.en: to make more beautiful by decorating

words.example: Flowers adorn the tables at the wedding.

gloss.en: done or given before something happens

words.example: Please give us advance warning of any changes.

gloss.en: the process of moving forward or improving

words.example: The job offers good chances for advancement.

gloss.en: the arrival of an important thing or event

words.example: Life changed with the advent of the internet.

gloss.en: willing to try new and exciting things

words.example: She is an adventurous traveller who loves new places.

gloss.en: in a harmful or negative way

words.example: The bad weather adversely affected the crops.

gloss.en: a difficult or unpleasant situation

words.example: She stayed calm in the face of adversity.

gloss.en: an event or matter of public interest

words.example: He likes to read about world affairs.

gloss.en: showing warm love or fondness

words.example: She gave him an affectionate hug.

gloss.en: in a loving and warm way

words.example: He smiled affectionately at his daughter.

gloss.en: cheap enough for people to buy
gloss.zh: 负担得起的；价格合理的

words.example: We offer quality products at affordable prices.

gloss.en: at a later time; after an event

words.example: Afterwards she was sorry for what she'd said.

gloss.en: never seeming to grow old

words.example: Her beauty seemed truly ageless.

gloss.en: a business that provides a service

words.example: She works for an advertising agency.

gloss.en: a list of things to discuss at a meeting

words.example: We have a very full agenda to discuss.

gloss.en: angry and ready to attack or fight

words.example: He becomes aggressive when he drinks.

gloss.en: in an angry or forceful way

words.example: The dog barked aggressively at strangers.

gloss.en: to worry or think very hard about something

words.example: She agonised for hours over the decision.

gloss.en: to worry or think very hard about something

words.example: He agonized over which job to take.

gloss.en: very great pain or suffering

words.example: He was in agony after he broke his leg.

gloss.en: the science or practice of farming

words.example: Many people in this region work in agriculture.

gloss.en: help such as money or food sent to others

words.example: The country sent food aid to the region.

gloss.en: a serious illness caused by the HIV virus

words.example: Doctors are still fighting the spread of aids.

gloss.en: a vehicle that can fly

words.example: The aircraft landed safely at the airport.

gloss.en: the people who fly and serve on a plane

words.example: The aircrew prepared the plane for takeoff.

gloss.en: a sound or signal that warns of danger

words.example: She decided to sound the alarm.

gloss.en: the illness of drinking too much alcohol

words.example: He is getting treatment for alcoholism.

gloss.en: next to or at the side of something

words.example: A police car pulled up alongside us.

gloss.en: arranged in the order of the alphabet

words.example: The names are listed in alphabetical order.

gloss.en: a holy table used in religious services

words.example: They placed candles on the altar.

gloss.en: to change, or make something different

words.example: Prices did not alter much during the year.

gloss.en: to happen in turns, one after the other

words.example: The seasons alternate throughout the year.

gloss.en: used to give a second choice or option

words.example: We could drive, or alternatively take the train.

gloss.en: the height of something above sea level

words.example: The plane is flying at a high altitude.

gloss.en: completely; in total

words.example: The train went slower and then stopped altogether.

gloss.en: a light, silver-coloured metal

words.example: The cans are made of aluminum.

gloss.en: to surprise someone very much

words.example: It never fails to amaze me how kind she is.

gloss.en: a feeling of great surprise

words.example: To my amazement, the plan actually worked.

gloss.en: in a surprising or wonderful way

words.example: Amazingly, no one was hurt in the crash.

gloss.en: having more than one possible meaning

words.example: There is some ambiguity in the contract.

gloss.en: having more than one possible meaning

words.example: His answer was vague and ambiguous.

gloss.en: a vehicle for taking sick people to hospital

words.example: Call an ambulance!

gloss.en: a strong-smelling gas or cleaning liquid

words.example: The cleaner smelled strongly of ammonia.

gloss.en: in the middle of; surrounded by

words.example: She felt safe amongst her friends.

gloss.en: a quantity of something

words.example: I was amazed at the amount he could eat.

gloss.en: enough or more than enough

words.example: There is ample time to finish the work.

gloss.en: a device that makes sounds louder

words.example: He plugged his guitar into the amplifier.

gloss.en: to make a sound or signal louder

words.example: They used a microphone to amplify his voice.

gloss.en: to make someone laugh or smile

words.example: His funny stories always amuse the children.

gloss.en: funny and giving pleasure

words.example: He told us an amusing story.

gloss.en: a person who studies facts to give views

words.example: She works as a financial analyst.

gloss.en: a family member from long ago

words.example: His ancestors came to America from Ireland.

gloss.en: a safe place where ships can anchor

words.example: The boats found a safe anchorage in the bay.

gloss.en: a man who presents a news programme

words.example: He works as an anchorman for the evening news.

gloss.en: a person who presents a news programme

words.example: The anchorperson reported the breaking news.

gloss.en: a strong feeling about something unfair

words.example: She had to find a way to express her anger.

gloss.en: the space where two lines meet, in degrees

words.example: The two lines meet at a right angle.

gloss.en: severe pain or mental suffering

words.example: Her face was full of anguish and fear.

gloss.en: to make drawings or models appear to move

words.example: They used software to animate the cartoon.

gloss.en: the making of films with moving drawings

words.example: The children love computer animation.

gloss.en: a yearly date marking a special event

words.example: They celebrated their wedding anniversary.

gloss.en: happening once every year

words.example: The company holds an annual meeting.

gloss.en: once a year

words.example: The exhibition is held annually.

gloss.en: used to attack enemy planes

words.example: The base was protected by antiaircraft guns.

gloss.en: able to kill or stop bacteria

words.example: She washed her hands with antibacterial soap.

gloss.en: used to fight or prevent cancer

words.example: Scientists are testing a new anticancer drug.

gloss.en: to expect something to happen

words.example: We don't anticipate any major problems.

gloss.en: a feeling of expecting something to happen

words.example: The crowd waited in eager anticipation.

gloss.en: meant to reduce or stop pollution

words.example: The city passed new antipollution laws.

gloss.en: old and often valuable because of its age

words.example: They sell antique furniture in that shop.

gloss.en: reducing the effects of static electricity

words.example: She used an antistatic spray on the carpet.

gloss.en: designed to protect against computer viruses

words.example: You should install antivirus software on your laptop.
